title: Apparatus, methods and systems for enabling a vehicle to anticipate irregularities in a travelling surface
abstract: Disclosed herein is a system for enabling a primary vehicle to anticipate irregularities in a travelling surface. The system comprises a scout vehicle that is configured to precede the primary vehicle by a distance that exceeds an emergency stopping distance of the primary vehicle and which transmits data indicative of a condition of a travelling surface over which the scout vehicle is traveling, and an adjustable suspension operably associated with the primary vehicle. The primary vehicle is configured to receive the data and, if the data is indicative of a traversable irregularity in the travelling surface, to activate the adjustable suspension whereby irregularities in the travelling surface are anticipated..
claims: Claims CLAIMS: 1. A system for enabling a primary vehicle to anticipate irregularities in a travelling surface, the system comprising: a scout vehicle that is configured to precede the primary vehicle by a distance that exceeds an emergency stopping distance of the primary vehicle, and which transmits data indicative of a condition of a travelling surface over which the scout vehicle is traveling; an adjustable suspension operably associated with the primary vehicle; the primary vehicle being configured to receive the data and, if the data is indicative of a traversable irregularity in the travelling surface, to activate the adjustable suspension whereby irregularities in the travelling surface are anticipated. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the data indicative of the condition of the travelling surface over which the scout vehicle is travelling includes a measure of an irregularity in the travelling surface over which the scout vehicle is travelling. 3. The system of claim 2, wherein the data indicative of the condition of the travelling surface over which the scout vehicle is travelling includes a profile of the measured irregularity. 4. The system of any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein the data transmitted by the scout vehicle includes a measure of a transverse force acting on the scout vehicle. 5. The system of any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the adjustable suspension comprises an air spring suspension system. 6. The system of claim 5, wherein air spring suspension system is located at wheels of the primary vehicle. 7. The system of claim 5 or claim 6, wherein a pressure inside the air spring suspension system is adjustable in anticipation of the irregularity, whereby a substantially constant pressure is maintained within the air suspension system as the irregularity is traversed. 8. The system of any one of clams 5 to 7, wherein the air spring suspension system is also activatable in response to data which is indicative of a transverse force acting on the scout vehicle. 9. The system of claim 8, wherein a pressure inside the air spring suspension of laterally opposing wheels of the primary vehicle is independently adjustable in anticipation of the transverse force. 10. The system of any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein the scout vehicle comprises: a sensor for sensing the condition of the travelling surface over which the scout vehicle is travelling; a transmitter for transmitting data indicative of the condition of the travelling surface over which the scout vehicle is travelling; a receiver for receiving data from the primary vehicle; and a controller which, in response to the data from the primary vehicle, controls a speed of the scout vehicle whereby the distance by which the scout vehicle precedes the primary vehicle exceeds an emergency stopping distance of the primary vehicle. 11. The system of any one of claim 10, wherein the sensor comprises an accelerometer. 12. The system of any one of claim 10 or 11, wherein the sensor comprises a gyroscope. 13. The system of any one of claims 1 to 12, wherein the primary vehicle is a train and the travelling surface is a rail line. 14. The system of any one of claims 1 to 13, wherein the primary vehicle is a very fast train (VFT) and the travelling surface is a rail line. 15. A vehicle suspension system for anticipating irregularities in a travelling surface, the suspension system comprising: a data receiver configured to receive data indicative of a condition of the travelling surface about to be traversed; a compressible member located between a cabin of the vehicle and the travelling surface, the compressibility of the compressible member being adjustable in anticipation of an irregularity in the travelling surface about to be traversed. 16. The suspension system of claim 15, wherein the compressible member comprises a fluid containing reservoir and a pump that is operable to add or remove fluid from the reservoir in anticipation of an irregularity in the travelling surface. 17. The suspension system of claim 15 or claim 16, wherein the compressible member comprises a plurality of fluid containing reservoirs, each reservoir being associable with a corresponding wheel of the vehicle. 18. The suspension system of any one of claims 15 to 17, wherein the data indicative of a condition of the travelling surface about to be traversed is transmitted by a scout vehicle travelling along the traveling surface ahead of the vehicle. 19. The suspension system of any one of claims 15 to 18, when used in the system for enabling a primary vehicle to anticipate irregularities in a travelling surface of any one of claims 1 to 14.
